summary: exact value or magnitude admission for a lexer-validated signless numeric token
requires: one successfully lexer-validated maximal digit-led token; sign is a separate PrefixExpr
exact optional target or magnitude-only request
preconditions: the source frontend emitted one valid numeric token and no lexical error
token parts and optional target are preserved
AST prefix sign is separate and unconsumed
normalization: classify the signless token once; preserve suffix; defaults are Int/Float64
decision_procedure: consume the already lexer-validated signless token and parse integer magnitude before target-range checking
magnitude-only mode returns an unsuffixed-integer magnitude fact to an enclosing direct-prefix adapter and never consumes -
normal mode selects exact suffix or representable Int/IntN/UIntN target for N={8,16,32,64,128}, never ISize/USize; default Int
unsuffixed float is Float64; Float32 needs f32
emit NUMERIC_LITERAL_OUT_OF_RANGE only for checker-owned representability failure; lexical failures never invoke this predicate
termination_metric: finite descriptor size plus strictly decreasing dependency DAG rank
evaluation_order: preconditions -> normalization -> dependencies -> local rule -> diagnostic

summary: intrinsic owner dispatch over exact literal results; failure precedes commit
requires: closed numeric glyph
exact operand domains
explicit place/dispatch
preconditions: closed intrinsic family
no provider/witness lookup
one place/RHS/commit slot
normalization: consume NumericLiteralAdmitted results; preserve domain, owner, and place
decision_procedure: reject nonintrinsic glyph dispatch with FIXED_OPERATOR_TRAIT_DISPATCH_NOT_CURRENT
delegate literals to NumericLiteralAdmitted first and propagate its diagnostic
scalar +,-,*,/ require one exact Int/Float domain; integer results are checked and / truncates toward zero
% integer-only: a=q*b+r, q toward zero, r zero/dividend-signed, |r|<|b|; float % is named
zero divisor or signed MIN/-1 /,% raises ArithmeticDefect precommit

^ requires one exact integer domain plus static nonnegative proof; negative/unknown proof emits NUMERIC_OPERATOR_CORE_REQUIRED; same-domain result is checked; Measure ^ StaticInt delegates measure_static_power_law; float pow named; NumericArray infix Preview
**/*+ delegate shape laws; bitwise needs exact known-width integer or finite bitfield/#flags domain
compound place/RHS run once; one success commit returns Unit
termination_metric: finite descriptor size plus strictly decreasing dependency DAG rank
evaluation_order: preconditions -> normalization -> dependencies -> local rule -> diagnostic

summary: ordinary mut acquires one argument into a callee-owned mutable local with no caller alias/writeback and retains the caller owner on precommit failure; borrow is a nonescaping shared region; inout is one exclusive caller-place borrow whose successful writes are visible to the caller; move transfers ownership once; cleanup remains with the current owner
requires: ordinary mut evaluates/acquires the argument once, commits one callee-owned mutable local, creates no caller alias/writeback edge, and retains the caller owner on precommit failure
borrow is shared, bounded by borrow_region, and does not escape
inout is exclusive for one dynamic call extent and overlaps no live borrow or inout region
move transfers ownership exactly once and every later use of the source is rejected
a live borrow/inout does not cross suspension, task escape, actor transfer, return, storage, or capture unless a separate admitted proof explicitly permits that boundary
cleanup responsibility remains attached to the current owner on every admitted path

replacement through an inout place is admitted by evaluating the replacement RHS, dropping the old place value, and installing the new value, but moving the current value out of an inout place is not current
